purpose: Interpretable per-word stylometric divergence between a target and a baseline corpus — rank-turbulence divergence (RTD, in [0,1]) over the function-word distribution by default. Where voice_distance reports one aggregate Burrows Delta, RTD's per-word contributions say WHICH words drive the difference (top contributors over-ranked in the target vs the baseline). Additive companion to voice_distance (does NOT modify it). Clean-room RTD (Dodds et al. 2020; stylometric-Delta adaptation arXiv:2604.19499); stdlib, deterministic. NOT a verdict: a high RTD is not "different author" or "AI"; topic/genre/length shift it (especially in --all-words mode). Claim-license refuses any authorship/AI inference; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want an interpretable, per-word complement to a voice_distance Burrows Delta run
You want to see which (function) words drive a target's divergence from a baseline
Target and baseline are >= ~200 words and register-matched
do_not_use_when: You want an authorship or AI-provenance verdict (this is a divergence measurement)
Target and baseline are topic-mismatched (the divergence becomes topical, not stylistic; --all-words mode is explicitly topical)
Either text is under ~200 words (unstable rank distribution)

inputs.baseline: a --baseline-dir of texts OR a --manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; no models. Deterministic; counting + ranking + arithmetic.

examples.description: Per-word RTD vs a baseline corpus (function words, the default)
Full-vocabulary (TOPICAL) mode with a custom alpha

references: Rank-Turbulence Delta — Interpretable Stylometric Delta (arXiv:2604.19499): https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19499
Allotaxonometry and rank-turbulence divergence — Dodds et al. 2020 (arXiv:2002.09770): https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.09770
